commit.message:
fix: provide freestanding memset/memcpy/memmove/memcmp for -nostdlib UEFI

AArch64 GCC emits calls to memset/memcpy for struct/array operations
even with -ffreestanding, and -nostdlib means there is no libc to
satisfy these references. Add common/mem.c with simple freestanding
implementations and include it in both UEFI x86_64 and AArch64 source
lists.

Also fix last remaining warning: mark linux_load 'info' parameter with
__attribute__((unused)) since the UEFI path delegates to EFI LoadImage
and doesn't populate BootInfo itself.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: integrate starter kits and spatial tool support (#509)

* feat(agent): add intent governance and validation-gated workflow build

* feat(frontend): surface governance plan and validation state in chat

* Fix React Flow edge handle resolution for example workflows

* Add Codex examples check command and remove legacy sweep

* Remove tool-node configuring indicator dot

* Fix disabled container execution using live canvas container state

* feat(workflow): add revision journal with autosave conflict checkpoints

* feat(workflow-xml): add embedded JS runtime and bundled bridge core

* feat(agent): enforce schema-first workflow edits and upstream hydration

* fix(canvas): preserve anchors and parent ordering in workflow conversion

* fix(tool-defs): align regex and generate-rows anchors

* chore(frontend): tighten governance payload cast in use-chat

* chore: align lint config and stabilize workflow schema/runtime tests

* Update root build pipeline and fix build warnings/errors

* fix(upstream-fields): refactor environment variable access for debug schema cascade

* Fix CreatePoints X/Y parsing and config panel parity

* Remove unintended sample workflow from previous commit

* fix(spatial-tools): add custom parsers and writers for TradeArea, SpatialInfo, SpatialMatch, SpatialProcess

Alteryx stores field values as XML attributes on child elements (e.g.
<SpatialObj field="Centroid" />) but the schema-driven parser was reading
text content, producing empty strings. The schema writer also produced
wrong XML (text content instead of attributes), causing engine errors like
"Could not find the required attribute 'field' in the node 'SpatialObj'".

Applies the same custom parser+writer pattern used for CreatePoints to
the four remaining broken spatial tools.

Changes:
- Add custom parsers: trade-area, spatial-info, spatial-match, spatial-process
- Add custom writers: trade-area, spatial-info, spatial-match, spatial-process
- spatial-process schema restructured: spatialObjFields → field1 + field2
  (XML has two distinct attributes; single field-picker was wrong)
- spatial-match parser reuses parseSelectConfiguration + handles
  outputConnection attr on nested <Configuration> element
- Add 22 parser unit tests across 4 new test files
- Register all 4 pairs in CUSTOM_PARSERS and CUSTOM_WRITERS registries
- Update JSON definitions with overrides.parser + overrides.writer

Verified: "02 Map a trade area around a store.yxmd" runs successfully
in neonAxe with all 4 tools producing correct output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Harden examples check instructions for live indexing and tab hygiene

* fix(mcp-schema): ensure Input Data field schemas are visible to agent

Three gaps closed so the agent can see source tool schemas after workflow
sync:

1. **XML round-trip for previewSchema** — `serializeInputDataConfig` now
   writes a `<PreviewSchema>` block when the schema is known; `parseInputData`
   reads it back. Schema no longer silently drops across the `raw_yxmd`
   round-trip that happens in `syncCanvasBeforeChat()`.

2. **Pre-agent enrichment** — `syncCanvasBeforeChat()` now calls
   `enrichInputDataSchemas()` before serialising. For any InputData node with
   a `filePath` but empty `previewSchema` and `metaInfo` (config panel was
   never opened), it reads column names from the Electron file-preview API and
   injects them as `previewSchema`. No-op in browser/test contexts.

3. **`workflow_get_node_output_schema` MCP tool** — new backend tool that lets
   the agent ask "what does node X emit?" after configuring it. Also hardens
   the bridge runtime to fail-fast instead of silently falling back to the
   `tsx` subprocess, and fixes `reconcileConfiguredNode` to read from canonical
   `raw_yxmd` rather than the stale `workflow_json` column.

Bridge bundle rebuilt to include the updated InputData parser.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-sync): agent node additions now persist through save and reopen

Three layered bugs prevented agent canvas mutations from surviving a
save/reopen cycle:

1. markSynced instability (use-canvas-sync): markSynced captured
   currentWorkflow in its useCallback deps, making it an unstable ref.
   Phase 1 of the post-stream reconciliation published config:saved →
   currentWorkflow changed → markSynced changed → the reconciliation
   useEffect re-ran → isMounted cleanup fired → Phase 2 getWorkflowDocument
   fetch was cancelled before replaceFromWorkflow could run. Fixed by
   reading currentWorkflow via a ref so markSynced has stable deps ([]).

2. useWorkflow never learned about agent changes (use-agent-integration,
   event-types, use-workflow): replaceFromWorkflow intentionally does not
   publish workflow:state-changed, so the page-level useWorkflow hook
   never updated its workflow state or latestVersionRef after agent runs.
   Manual save then wrote the stale pre-agent workflow to the backend.
   Fixed by publishing workflow:state-changed with backendVersion and
   assetId from the reconciliation pass; useWorkflow handles it by
   updating state and version without marking the workflow dirty.

3. Unstable workflow ID filter (use-workflow): workflow:state-changed
   events were filtered by event.workflow.id === workflowIdRef.current.
   For YXMD files lacking a <WorkflowId> element, each parseWorkflow()
   call generates a fresh UUID, so the reconciliation event's projected.id
   never matched the initial load's UUID and was silently dropped. Fixed
   by including assetId in agent-sourced events and matching by assetId
   (stable) for those events, falling back to workflow.id for user edits.

Bonus: save() now uses assetNameRef (populated from asset.name on load)
as the fallback for workflow:name-changed, preventing the title bar from
clearing on workflows where metadata.name is empty (nameIsFileName=true).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-chat): inject line break between agentic turns to prevent text smashing

Without this fix, consecutive utterances from multi-turn agent responses
(e.g. "Let me check...The schema shows...") were concatenated with no
separator. Now a double newline is injected on turn_start events for turn 2+.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(mcp): add tests for Summarize config alias normalization

Adds 7 unit tests for normalize_summarize_config covering:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(canvas): collapse node+edge deletion into single undo step

When deleting a connected node, React Flow fires onNodesChange and
onEdgesChange as separate calls. Both previously called pushState,
creating a spurious intermediate history entry that required 3 undos
instead of 1 to fully restore the deleted node and its connections.

Fix: move removal logic to the synchronous top of onNodesChange (before
any setNodes updater), proactively remove orphaned edges, populate a
suppression ref so the subsequent onEdgesChange skips pushState, then
issue a single atomic pushState covering both node and edge removals.

Also fix undo restoration: canvasNodesToWorkflow skips nodes absent from
workflowRef.current, causing restored nodes to silently drop from the
workflow model (broken config panel click). Patch workflowRef.current
with reconstructed WorkflowNodes from ToolNodeData before conversion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(select): write canonical sizes for fixed-type SelectFields to prevent engine size errors

When the Select tool applies a type override (e.g. DateTime, Double, Int32) to an upstream
field from a CSV source, the Alteryx engine inherits the upstream V_WString(254) buffer size
rather than the type's natural size — causing "Size of 254 for 'DateTime' fields is out of
bounds" errors at run time.

Fixes:
- select-fields-helper.ts: always write canonical size (e.g. size="19" for DateTime) in
  <SelectField> XML for fixed-size types; skip size for variable-size types unless user set one
- serialize-node.ts: same guard in <MetaInfo><Field> blocks so serialised schemas are clean
- select-output-columns.tsx (resolveInitialFields): don't copy upstream FieldInfo size for
  fixed-size types when auto-populating the Select panel on first open
- use-select-field-rows.ts: emit a config update on panel mount when stored fields are empty
  so running without opening the panel no longer sends fields:[] to the engine
- Rebuilt shared/dist/workflow-xml-runtime.bundle.js to include serializer fixes
- workflow-editor.tsx / use-workflow-execution.ts: wire usePresence and assetId for browse
  query relay and run log tagging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(select): fall back to *Unknown pass-through when SelectFields is empty

A freshly-added Select tool has fields:[] in its configuration until the
config panel is opened. Serialising an empty fields array produces an empty
<SelectFields /> which the Alteryx engine rejects with "No fields have been
selected for output".

serializeSelectConfiguration now substitutes [{ field: '*Unknown', selected: true }]
when fields is empty, so the engine passes all upstream fields through by default —
the same behaviour as a Select tool that has never been configured in Alteryx Designer.

Rebuilt shared/dist/workflow-xml-runtime.bundle.js to include the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent): prevent Axe mid-workflow abandonment on Formula misconfiguration

Root cause: LLM used fieldName/dataType instead of field/type in Formula
config. xml-builder's String(undefined) wrote field="undefined" to XML,
which schema transforms treated as a real field name, causing the agent
to spiral debugging a phantom problem. The agent also configured Summarize
before connecting it, failing with Unknown field(s) it never recovered from.

- formula.ts: skip FormulaField entries where field.field is falsy — prevents
  field="undefined" from corrupting the workflow XML
- workflow_configure_node.py: new _validate_formula_field_property_names()
  raises an actionable ValidationError when fieldName or dataType are used;
  also updated _TOOL description with explicit Formula example
- prompts.py: add Rule 13 (connect-before-configure order) and Rule 14
  (return to plan and retry after investigating a failure)
- runtime.py: increase max_turns default 10 → 20 so complex multi-node
  workflows have budget for recovery after a single failure
- tests: formula writer guard + Formula property name validation tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(chat): render markdown tables and GFM formatting in agent responses

Install remark-gfm and add table component overrides (table, thead,
tbody, tr, th, td) to chatMarkdownComponents. Pass remarkPlugins to all

- New workflows named "New Workflow N" (uniquified per open tabs) instead of bare "New Workflow"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fixes two bugs where NaxAssetSaveDialog was not triggered:

2. Save button bug: WorkflowEditorPage now reads the active tab name via

Supporting changes:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(workflow): clear stale tab dirty-state on clean load and fix output-data field name

Previously, saves could silently fail (e.g. serializer crash when a
config field was undefined), leaving workflow:dirty-changed { isDirty: false }
unpublished. The tab's isDirty: true would then persist to localStorage.
On next app open the tab was restored as dirty, and since use-workflow only
published workflow:dirty-changed when hasUnsavedChanges was true, the stale
dirty state was never cleared.

Fix: publish workflow:dirty-changed unconditionally after loading finishes,
so a clean fresh load always resets any stale localStorage tab dirty state.

Also rename the output-data config default field from 'delimeter' (typo) to
'delimiters' and update the bundled runtime.

commit.message:
Fix .gitconfig.lock race in Configure Git task on Windows (#291)

* Initial plan

* fix: force sequential git config writes to avoid .gitconfig.lock race

The Configure Git task previously processed all git config settings in
parallel via Rayon. On Windows, git2 uses .gitconfig.lock when writing
to the global config, so concurrent writes raced on the lock file and
all but the first writer failed with:

  failed to create locked file '.gitconfig.lock': The file exists.

Fix:
- Add `sequential: bool` field to ProcessOpts (false by default)
- Add ProcessOpts::sequential() builder method
- Gate parallel dispatch on `!opts.sequential` in orchestrate.rs
- Use ProcessOpts::strict(...).sequential() in the Configure Git task

fix: filter atom_ IDs from BFS — mem_ compound hubs have no content

The edges table connects mem_ (compound hubs) → atom_ nodes.
BFS visited set contained both types, but fetchNodes only queries atoms,
so 1817 of 1822 results had no match → only 5 atoms returned.

Fix: filter nodeIds to atom_-prefixed IDs after BFS completes.
The mem_ IDs are still used for BFS traversal (correct) but excluded
from the final content fetch.

accumulate weight from their incoming mem_ → atom_ edges, so scoring
still works correctly for importance ordering.

Phase 4.3: Add instruction lowering foundation for Intel EU backend

Implements the foundational instruction lowering infrastructure for Phase 4.3
(Intel EU Backend). This commit establishes the framework for translating
naga IR operations to Intel EU machine code.

New Module: render-sys/src/eu/lower.rs (412 LOC)
- LoweringContext: Manages instruction generation from naga IR
- Binary arithmetic lowering: Add, Subtract (via negation), Multiply
- Unary arithmetic lowering: Negate, BitwiseNot, LogicalNot
- Expression-to-register mapping
- Automatic register allocation during lowering
- 9 comprehensive tests covering all implemented operations

Enhanced Modules:
- instruction.rs: Added mutable setter methods for imperative instruction building
- encoding.rs: Added NEGATE and ABSOLUTE source modifier constants
- regalloc.rs: Added allocate_vreg() and get_physical() helper methods
- README.md: Updated status, metrics, and next steps

Test Results:
- All 138 Rust tests passing (up from 130)
- All Go tests passing (cached)
- Zero regressions in Go code metrics
- 100% test coverage for new lowering API

Integration:
- Declared in eu/mod.rs as `pub mod lower`
- Ready for next iteration: extended operations, type system, vectorization

Next Steps (Phase 4.3 continuation):
- Extended instruction lowering (division, math functions, comparisons)
- Type system integration (vectors, matrices, conversions)
- Texture sampling (SEND instructions)
- I/O handling (URB reads/writes, uniforms)

Reference: ROADMAP.md Phase 4.3, Intel PRMs Volume 4 (EU ISA)

feat: 30+ reactive hooks, router crate, comprehensive API docs

New reactive utilities (oxide-core):
  - watch(source, callback) — react to signal changes, skips initial
  - on_mount(f) — run after current microtask completes
  - on_cleanup(f) — register cleanup for beforeunload

New reactive hooks (oxide-dom):
  - use_local_storage(key, default) — persistent signal
  - use_interval(ms) — reactive timer tick
  - use_debounce(signal, ms) — debounced output signal
  - use_throttle(signal, ms) — throttled output signal
  - use_window_size() — reactive (width, height)
  - use_scroll() — reactive document scroll position
  - use_mouse() — reactive global mouse position
  - use_media_query(query) — reactive CSS media query
  - use_online() — network online/offline status
  - use_preferred_dark() — prefers-color-scheme signal
  - use_click_outside(el, handler) — click outside detection
  - use_focus(el) — focus tracking signal
  - set_title(title) — set document title
  - set_meta(name, content) — set/create meta tags

New crate: oxide-router — client-side routing:
  - Router::new(mode, routes) — hash or history mode
  - route(path, view) — define routes with :param segments
  - router.view() — reactive outlet that swaps views
  - navigate(path) — programmatic navigation
  - link(href, text) / nav_link() — client-side links
  - use_route() — current path signal
  - use_param(name) / use_params() — route parameters

Docs rewrite (docs.html):
  - 16 categorized sections with table of contents
  - ~90 APIs documented with code examples
  - Categories: Getting Started, CLI, Core Reactivity, View Macro,
    Reactive Hooks, DOM Utils, Head Management, Routing, Timers,
    Storage, Console, Location, OTel, Resiliency, Components, Debugging

Framework now has 9 crates, ~90 public APIs.

commit.message:
fix: replace full-response hash with coarse structural token hash

The full normalized-response hash treated every distinct QMP argument
combination as unique coverage (e.g., device-list-properties(e1000) vs
device-list-properties(rtl8139) -> different hashes despite same behavior
shape), causing 97.7% per-execution corpus add rate and 10M+ queue files
after 24h of fuzzing.

New approach: classify each QMP response line into one of ~12 stable
structural tokens (R0/RA/RO/RS for success variants, E:ClassName for
errors, V:EventName for async events). Hash the sorted multiset of tokens.
This caps practical corpus size to ~10K-50K and eliminates fine-grained
content-based false diversity. Verified: 956 entries after 500 iters.

Additional changes:
- Add thread-id: to SPACED_PATTERNS (normalizes query-cpus-fast OS PID)
- Add qmp_response_token() + find_bytes() helper functions
- Add op 17: qom-set (write QOM properties, exercises property-setter paths)
  with 10 test values: true/false/0/1/255/65535/4294967295/string/null
- Update ChunkOpMutator to below(18)
- Add seeds: seed-qom-set.bin, seed-qom-set-int.bin

commit.message:
fix: add -no-pie to BIOS link flags

Modern GCC defaults to PIE even with -fno-pic at compile time, causing
R_X86_64_32S relocation errors when linking the freestanding BIOS stage2
binary. Add -no-pie to both CMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS_INIT in the bios
toolchain and target_link_options in the bios target to force a plain
non-PIE executable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
